report_name: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments

threat_actors.name: Volt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: A persistent threat actor mentioned as a key driver for the implementation of Zero Trust principles in Operational Technology (OT) environments to secure critical infrastructure.

identities.name: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: Lead U.S. government agency that, in partnership with other agencies, published the joint guide for implementing Zero Trust principles in Operational Technology (OT) environments.

identities.name: Department of War
identities.description: A U.S. government partner in the publication of the 'Adapting Zero Trust Principles to Operational Technology' guide.

identities.name: Department of Energy
identities.description: A U.S. government partner in the publication of the 'Adapting Zero Trust Principles to Operational Technology' guide.

identities.name: FBI
identities.description: A U.S. government partner in the publication of the 'Adapting Zero Trust Principles to Operational Technology' guide.

identities.name: Department of State
identities.description: A U.S. government partner in the publication of the 'Adapting Zero Trust Principles to Operational Technology' guide.

identities.name: NIST
identities.description: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0 is referenced as the structural basis for the Zero Trust in OT guidance.

identities.name: U.S. Critical Infrastructure Sectors
identities.description: The target audience for the CISA-led Zero Trust for OT guidance, encompassing owners and operators of Operational Technology systems in sectors such as energy, water, manufacturing, and transportation.
